spec_match.rationale: The page now exercises real CSS custom function parsing and computed behavior for @function, dashed function calls, typed <color>/<percentage>/<number> parameters, default values, returns <color>, local variables, call-site custom properties, nested custom function calls, and typed-argument rejection.

interactive.rationale: The live tool exposes token selection plus numeric controls for lighten, darken, mix, and alpha, and those controls update CSS variables consumed by custom functions rather than repainting the swatches directly in JavaScript.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still demonstrates color tools built from CSS custom functions and now matches the feature page's parameter/default/function framing more closely.

edge_cases.rationale: Visible edge cases cover 0% and 100% boundaries, alpha 0 transparency, defaulted parameters, call-site variables, local variables, and invalid typed arguments.

references.rationale: The page includes the required ChromeStatus feature 5179721933651968 link and the CSSWG custom functions spec link.

design_system.rationale: Raw source hex values and static inline styles were removed; colors come from shared design tokens or runtime CSS variables, and local button background overrides also set color.

summary: The color tools concept now clears its previous spec, edge-case, reference, and design-system gaps.
